title: amellus novi-belgii
intro: Amellus novi-belgii, also known as the New York aster, is a perennial plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it effective in treating a range of health issues. The plant's flowers, leaves, and roots are used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory infections, as well as to promote digestion and relieve menstrual cramps. Additionally, the plant's antiseptic properties make it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions, such as eczema and acne.
